title: Andrew Hall is on a roll - Marginal REVOLUTION
gist: Anti-billionaire rhetoric, long a niche of Democratic messaging, quadrupled after January 2025 and is now a mainstay, peaking above 20% of Democratic fundraising emails.

insights.claim: Through the Biden presidency, billionaires appeared in only about one of every twenty to twenty-five Democratic fundraising emails, barely more than in Republican ones, making anti-billionaire language a niche product.
insights.speaker: Tyler Cowen
insights.so_what: Establishes a low baseline against which the post-2025 surge is a sharp regime change in political rhetoric toward wealth.
insights.quote: the billionaire appeared in roughly one of every twenty to twenty-five Democratic fundraising emails, barely more than in Republican ones
insights.locator: For most of the past decade

insights.claim: After January 2025, following an inauguration seating tech CEOs in the front row and Elon Musk's DOGE experiment, billionaire mentions in Democratic emails quadrupled, peaking above 20% and holding around 15% since.
insights.speaker: Tyler Cowen
insights.so_what: A durable shift toward anti-wealth populist messaging signals rising political risk for visible billionaires and their companies heading into future cycles.
insights.quote: billionaire mentions in Democratic emails quadrupled, peaking above 20 percent of all emails sent and holding around 15 percent ever since
